economic_notes: Forestry operations through Obishikokaang Resources Corporation; Tim Hortons franchise through Waaninaawagaang Developments LP and 2361701 Ontario Inc.; Lac Seul Generating Station Limited Partnership (25% interest); Shared Spirits Limited Partnership; Landmark Inn Limited Partnership; Indigenous Transmission Limited Partnership; Sioux Lookout Friendship Accord Limited Partnership; joint venture/exploration revenue $3,266,182; silviculture and roads revenue $2,280,943; internet revenue $630,646; biomass heating plants under construction; elders assisted living under construction; lagoon project under construction $9,258,591.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to improper deferral of program revenue surpluses — auditor states that if revenue had not been deferred, deferred revenue would decrease by $22,372,361, accumulated surplus would increase by $22,372,361, and annual surplus would decrease by $2,403,559. Highly complex consolidation with 13 fully consolidated subsidiaries including housing authorities, resources corporation, Tim Hortons LP, trust entity, and generating station partnership. Flood Claim Settlement trust established from Government of Canada compensation. Per capita payment of $1,250 to eligible members ($4,275,000 total estimated payout) ongoing. Auditor firm name given only as 'Chartered Professional Accountants Inc.' located in Winnipeg, Manitoba — no firm trade name provided. expenses_housing includes Ke-nawind Housing Development Authority ($945,663) and Lac Seul CMHC Housing Authority ($1,773,843). expenses_enterprises includes Obishikokaang Resources Corporation ($2,397,935), 2361701 Ontario Inc. ($1,875), Waaninaawagaang Developments LP ($63,439), and Ontario First Nations Limited Partnership ($708,947). No remuneration schedule present in extracted text.
